notes: Works out of the box quite well.
title: Trine 4: The Nightmare Prince

notes: The game works out of the box. the game seemed to auto detect the correct graphical settings for my PC.  my default settings were 2k for textures and medium TAA for antialiasing. When I tried to increase these settings the visuals started to lag and stutter.  so its best to keep the settings as they are.
title: Trine 4: The Nightmare Prince

notes: Steam Controller not detected. Crash one time after about one hour of gameplay, then I restart the game and continue it for about another 30 minutes.
title: Trine 4: The Nightmare Prince

notes: played tutorial and some levels after that
title: Trine 4: The Nightmare Prince

notes: Game was capped at 30 fps. Only after I changed "setOption(renderingModule, "OnBatteryFramerateLimit", 30)" to "setOption(renderingModule, "OnBatteryFramerateLimit", 60)" in steamapps/compatdata/690640/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Application Data/Trine4/options.txt I got 60 fps. I also made a snippet video of the game on Proton: https://youtu.be/4q7T0Sc3M3A
title: Trine 4: The Nightmare Prince

notes:
Given the visual quality of the game, I should expect the game to easily run at 60+ FPS on my hardware, but it's locked at 30 FPS. This may be because of it's supposed to maintain this cap on the Nintendo Switch but instead they perhaps implemented the cap as a default for non-Windows platforms. Playable, but a visually handicapped experience in a game that's known for its visuals.
Another problem is that changing graphics settings in-game (especially the texture settings) can cause the game and even the OS to freeze. This did not happen when I was in the main menu.
Besides these two problems, the game is playable. I'm about an hour into the game as of writing this and have not run into other bugs so far.
title: Trine 4: The Nightmare Prince

notes: No further configuration was required to run the game. No graphical error or loss of performance.
